refine: {"title":"essay on ambition in macbeth, need it by friday","description":null,"subject":"English Literature","intent":"essay","priority":"hard","deadline":"2026-07-15T17:00:00Z","tags":["extra-credit","group-project"]} steps: ["Reread the key ambition scenes (1.7, 2.2, 5.5) and jot down what changes in Macbeth","Pick 3 quotes that track his ambition from spark to ruin","Write the essay","Outline: intro, one paragraph per quote/stage, conclusion","Draft the essay (~1000 words)","Check every paragraph actually analyses language, not just plot"] answers: [{"question":"What's your overall argument about how ambition changes Macbeth?","answer":"it starts as ambition for the crown but becomes a compulsive need for control once he can't undo the murder"}]

target: ["Reproduce the segfault reliably with the smallest input that triggers it","Run it under a debugger (gdb) or with valgrind to get the exact crashing line","Check whether it's a null pointer dereference, out-of-bounds access, or use-after-free","Narrow down why the pointer is invalid at that point by tracing backward","Fix it, then re-run under valgrind again to confirm no other memory errors remain","Add a bounds/null check where appropriate so this fails loudly instead of silently next time"]

refine: {"title":"segfault in my C program, debugging existing code","description":null,"subject":"Computer Science","intent":"coding","priority":"hard","deadline":"2026-07-15T17:00:00Z","tags":["group-project"]} steps: ["Reproduce the segfault reliably with the smallest input that triggers it","Run it under a debugger (gdb) or with valgrind to get the exact crashing line","Research the topic","Narrow down why the pointer is invalid at that point by tracing backward","Fix it, then re-run under valgrind again to confirm no other memory errors remain","Add a bounds/null check where appropriate so this fails loudly instead of silently next time"] answers: [{"question":"What did gdb/valgrind actually report as the crashing line and error type?","answer":"valgrind says invalid read of size 4, looks like I'm accessing an array one past its end"}]
